metadata.description: Pleiotropic perturbations: each test asserts outcomes across 2+ phenotype nodes.
metadata.flowering_time_convention: propensity (higher = earlier flowering); flipped from archived days-to-flower labels

pleiotropic_tests.description: DELLA pentuple mutant (gai rga rgl1 rgl2 rgl3) removes all DELLA growth repressors

evidence.title: DELLA genes restrict inflorescence meristem function independently of plant height
evidence.evidence_sentence: Semi-dwarf DELLA mutants with improved harvest index and decreased lodging greatly improved global food security. DELLA genes restrict inflorescence meristem function independently of plant height.
evidence.claim: DELLA mutants show pleiotropic effects: tall stature, elongated hypocotyls, early flowering

pleiotropic_tests.description: phyB mutant shows constitutive shade avoidance phenotype

evidence.title: Functional analysis of PHYB polymorphisms in Arabidopsis thaliana collected in Patagonia
evidence.evidence_sentence: Flowering was accelerated in phyB mutants. Like phyB mutants, elf3 mutants have elongated hypocotyls and petioles, flower early. Several phytochrome mutants had many fewer branches consistent with constitutive shade avoidance phenotype.
evidence.claim: phyB mutants have elongated hypocotyls, flower early, and show reduced branching

pleiotropic_tests.description: bri1 brassinosteroid-insensitive mutant has multiple growth defects

evidence.title: A brassinosteroid-insensitive mutant in Arabidopsis thaliana exhibits multiple defects in growth and development
evidence.evidence_sentence: The bri1 mutant exhibited a severely dwarfed stature, dark green thickened leaves, male sterility, reduced apical dominance, and de-etiolation of dark-grown seedlings.
evidence.claim: bri1 mutant shows severely dwarfed stature, reduced apical dominance, and smaller seeds

evidence.title: Brassinosteroid Regulates Seed Size and Shape in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The bri1-116 seeds were 18% lighter than those of the wild type.
evidence.claim: bri1-116 seeds were 18% lighter than wild type

evidence.title: Brassinosteroid Regulates Seed Size and Shape in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR-deficient mutants display a dwarf phenotype. The det2 seeds were 17% lighter than the wild type. The embryos from mature det2 seeds were significantly smaller than those of the wild type.
evidence.claim: det2 mutant is dwarf with short hypocotyls and smaller seeds

pleiotropic_tests.description: spy mutant mimics constitutive GA signaling

evidence.title: Mutations at the SPINDLY locus of Arabidopsis alter gibberellin signal transduction
evidence.evidence_sentence: Relative to wild type, spy mutants exhibit longer hypocotyls, increased stem elongation, early flowering, parthenocarpy, and partial male sterility. All of these phenotypes are also observed when wild-type plants are repeatedly treated with GA3.
evidence.claim: spy mutants show early flowering, longer hypocotyls, and increased stem elongation

pleiotropic_tests.description: ga1 gibberellin biosynthesis mutant is GA-deficient

evidence.title: Gibberellins control Arabidopsis hypocotyl growth via regulation of cellular elongation
evidence.evidence_sentence: Severely GA-deficient mutants such as ga1 are extreme dwarfs with much reduced hypocotyl and root lengths and small rosette leaves.
evidence.claim: ga1 mutant is an extreme dwarf with reduced hypocotyl length

evidence.title: PIF4: Integrator of light and temperature cues in plant growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 was identified as a central role in promoting plant hypocotyl growth via the activation of auxin synthesis genes. PIF4 regulates hypocotyl elongation, flowering, and various light and temperature responses.
evidence.claim: pif4 mutants display short hypocotyls and delayed flowering under warm temperature

pleiotropic_tests.description: hy5 mutant shows elongated hypocotyl and increased lateral root initiation

evidence.title: HY5 inhibits lateral root initiation in Arabidopsis through negative regulation of the microtubule-stabilizing protein TPXL5
evidence.evidence_sentence: HY5 inhibits lateral root initiation in Arabidopsis through negative regulation of TPXL5. The hy5 mutant exhibits higher lateral root density.
evidence.claim: hy5 has elongated hypocotyl and increased lateral root density

pleiotropic_tests.description: max2 strigolactone signaling mutant shows increased branching and altered root architecture

evidence.title: Strigolactones affect lateral root formation and root-hair elongation in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The max2 mutant exhibits increased branching. Strigolactones affect lateral root formation and root-hair elongation in Arabidopsis.
evidence.claim: max2 mutant shows increased shoot branching and altered lateral root development

pleiotropic_tests.description: ahk2 ahk3 cytokinin receptor double mutant

evidence.title: Arabidopsis Cytokinin Receptor Mutants Reveal Functions in Shoot Growth, Leaf Senescence, Seed Size, Germination, Root Development, and Cytokinin Metabolism
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ahk2 ahk3 double mutants exhibited a semidwarf phenotype with final height about two-thirds of wild type. More than twice the number of side roots compared with wild type.
evidence.claim: ahk2 ahk3 double mutant shows reduced height and increased lateral root number

evidence.title: Brassinosteroid Regulates Seed Size and Shape in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: dwf4 seeds were 17% lighter than those of the wild type. BR biosynthetic mutants display a dwarf phenotype.
evidence.claim: dwf4 mutant shows dwarfism and reduced seed weight

pleiotropic_tests.description: ein2 ethylene insensitive mutant affects root development and flowering

evidence.title: Ethylene regulates lateral root formation and auxin transport in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mutations that block ethylene responses, like ein2-5, enhance root formation. ein2-5 suppresses the phenotypes of early flowering.
evidence.claim: ein2 mutants show enhanced root formation and delayed flowering

pleiotropic_tests.description: taa1 tar2 auxin biosynthesis double mutant

evidence.title: The main auxin biosynthesis pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The taa1 tar2 double mutants display pleiotropic phenotypes in both roots and shoots. The Arabidopsis double mutant of TAA1 and TAR2 produces dwarf, bushy plants with agravitropic roots, reduced vasculature, and sterile flowers.
evidence.claim: taa1 tar2 double mutants produce dwarf bushy plants with reduced root development

evidence.title: The GSK3-like kinase BIN2 phosphorylates and destabilizes BZR1, a positive regulator of the brassinosteroid signaling pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BZR1 is a positive regulator of the brassinosteroid signaling pathway. BZR1 directly targets growth-promoting genes including those controlling hypocotyl elongation and stem growth.
evidence.claim: BZR1 overexpression promotes cell elongation in hypocotyls and stems

evidence.title: BIN2 Functions Redundantly with Other Arabidopsis GSK3-Like Kinases to Regulate Brassinosteroid Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIN2 gain-of-function mutants are BR-insensitive dwarfs with dark-green downward-curled leaves. Growth of bin2-1 mutants rescued their short hypocotyl phenotype.
evidence.claim: BIN2 gain-of-function mutants are BR-insensitive dwarfs with short hypocotyls
